definition: Credit markets are directionally underpricing risk relative to rates and equity-implied measures. Residual risk premium is 0-80bp vs post-2000 median of 180-220bp (bottom decile). However, after composition adjustment, net mispricing is 30-80bp rather than the 100-150bp some analyses imply.
evidence: Spread decomposition shows residual risk premium in bottom decile of post-2000 distribution
Equity-implied spreads (Merton model) 20-40bp wider than actual
Debate pair_0: both generalists agreed credit is the outlier
After composition adjustment net mispricing likely 30-80bp not 100-150bp
open_questions: What is composition-adjusted residual mispricing after addback correction?
Does central bank put account for the remaining residual?
